member.name: Sir Alec Shelbrooke
member.fullTitle: Rt Hon Sir Alec Shelbrooke MP
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Sir Alec Shelbrooke is the Conservative MP for Wetherby and Easingwold , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: RAF Linton-on-Ouse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, can she confirm that she will submit a formal planning application to North Yorkshire Council should the she decide to use former RAF Linton-on-Ouse for asylum accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No final decision has been made on whether to use Linton-on-Ouse as asylum accommodation. All the necessary assessments, planning permissions and approvals would be completed and properly considered before a decision is made.

writtenQuestions.heading: Future Combat Air System: Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what is the total spend to date on the Tempest project, including pre-concept and early concept phases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since its inception, the UK Government has invested over £2 billion on the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) Tempest project. In addition to this, the project has attracted over £700 million of private investment since the programme began.

writtenQuestions.heading: Type 83 Destroyers: Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what is the total spend to date on the Type 83 Destroyer project, including pre-concept and early concept phases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We inherited an underdeveloped concept for Type 83 when we took office. The Type 83 Destroyer forms part of a wider Future Air Dominance System (FADS) capability concept, making specific costs for the Type 83 element hard to differentiate from the wider w...

writtenQuestions.heading: Arts: Antisemitism
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what recent discussions she has had with the Charity Commission regarding antisemitism in the arts sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All forms of racial and religious discrimination are completely unacceptable and have no place in our arts charities. The Secretary of State recently met with the Charity Commission for England and Wales to discuss how to make sure it has the powers it ne...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Charities: Iran
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what recent discussions she has had with the Charity Commission regarding Iran-linked charities operating in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Charity Commission has published guidance for charities with links to Iran to be clear that charities must ensure any activity furthers their charity’s purposes and complies with the law and its guidance, or else face regulatory consequences.Governmen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Democracy and Human Rights
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, with reference to pages 19 to 20 of the Sixth Special Report of the Joint Committee on Human Rights of Session 2024-26 on Transnational repression in the UK: Government Respo
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office is working on a Human Rights and Democracy Report and will confirm publication timeframes in due course.

writtenQuestions.heading: Developing Countries: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what support is available for UK-based not-for-profit organisations developing innovative health technologies for use in the developing world.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) routes funding for the development of innovative health technologies for use in low and middle income countries through public-private Product Development Partnerships (PDPs) or similar organisations...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of heavier vehicles on the durability of materials used in the repair of road potholes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that heavier vehicles can accelerate road surface wear. While the Department has not undertaken a specific assessment of this effect, we are actively considering the implications of greater numbers of zero emission heavy goods ve...

writtenQuestions.heading: State Retirement Pensions: Income Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the Answer of 4 March 2026 to Question 115363, if she will provide the underlying data used to calculate the impact of the Personal Allowance freeze on those of state pension age.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Information on the methodology used to estimate the impacts of maintaining the personal income tax thresholds can be found in HM Treasury’s Policy Costing paper. Budget_2025-Policy_Costings.pdf The Chancellor has said that those whose only income is the b...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pensioners: Income Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate she has made of the cumulative number of pensioners who will be liable for income tax between 2026 and 2031 as a result of the the Personal Allowance threshold being frozen; and what assessment she has
writtenQuestions.answerText: The number of people forecast to pay tax by marginal rate can be found in Table 3.19 in the OBR’s November 2025 Economic and fiscal outlook – detailed forecast tables: receipts, linked below: https://obr.uk/download/november-2025-economic-and-fiscal-outlo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoner Escorts: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, if he will set out the oversight mechanisms in place to hold private custody and prisoner transport providers accountable for delays in delivering prisoners to court.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Prisoner Escort and Custody Services (PECS) are performing strongly, despite continuing pressures across the criminal justice system. In 2025, overall criminal justice system delivery to court was timely in 98.19% of cases; PECS suppliers met contractual ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Council Tax: Exemptions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of council tax exemption and discount rules for families required to live away from their homes for extended periods due to a child un
writtenQuestions.answerText: Councils are responsible for administering the council tax system, including considering the circumstances applying to a household and its eligibility for a discount or exemption. As well as the mandatory 25% discount where a household only has one adult ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy Drinks: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what consultation has his Department undertaken with vending and automated retailing businesses and other relevant stakeholders before proposing restrictions on the sale of high-energy caffeine dri
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department engaged with relevant stakeholders, including representatives of the vending and automated retail sector, prior to publishing its proposals for banning the sale of high-caffeine energy drinks.Building on initial engagements, the Department ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospices: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to implement a sustainable funding model for independent adult hospices.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have provided a £125 million capital funding boost for eligible adult, and children and young people’s, hospices in England to ensure they have the best physical environment for care.The Government is developing a Palliative Care and End-of-Life Care M...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Gaza: Ceasefires
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential implications for her policies of reports of Palestinian civilian deaths since the announcement of a ceasefire in Gaza.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We urge all parties to respect the terms of the ceasefire that came into effect on 10 October 2025, and we continue to press for the implementation in full of the 20-point peace plan endorsed by the United Nations Security Council on 17 November 2025.

writtenQuestions.heading: Plastic Packaging Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will review the scope of the Plastics Packaging Tax to exempt EN 13432–certified compostable materials; and what assessment has been made of the potential impact of including compostable materials within the
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government keeps all taxes under review as part of the policymaking process. The Plastic Packaging Tax provides a price incentive for businesses to use recycled plastic in the manufacture of plastic packaging.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cancer: Yorkshire and the Humber
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department made an assessment of trends in the levels of current waiting times for cancer treatment in NHS Humber and North Yorkshire and NHS West Yorkshire sub-ICBs prior to funding de
writtenQuestions.answerText: Cancer patients are waiting too long for diagnosis and treatment on the National Health Service, including in Humber and North Yorkshire and NHS West Yorkshire. The Government is investing an extra £26 billion in the NHS and opening up community diagnosti...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Offences against Children: Criminal Investigation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what mechanisms exist to ensure public safeguarding of victims when police investigations into child sexual exploitation are paused due to the mental health needs of those victims.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is crucial police and law enforcement have the capabilities and skills to effectively tackle child sexual abuse and exploitation and take robust action to better safeguard children, ensuring victims and survivors receive appropriate care and support th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Independent National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if his Department will publish (a) the long list of NHS trusts considered by the Chair for inclusion in the Independent Maternity and Neonatal Investigation and (b) the selection criteria used by t
writtenQuestions.answerText: The 14 NHS trusts to be looked at as part of the independent National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation were announced on 15 September 2025. There are no plans to publish the long list of NHS trusts considered by the chair for inclusion in the investig...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Independent National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, who has been selected to represent (a) midwifery, (b) obstetrics and (c) neonatal care for his Independent Maternity and Neonatal Investigation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The list of Expert Advisers supporting Baroness Amos is published in the terms of reference. They include:a) Professor Julia Sanders and Lesley Sharkey, both midwives.b) Professor Alex Heazell and Dr Christine Ekechi, both obstetricians.c) Dr Edile Murdoc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
